pi_items.source: task.json description field and export_result.sh LIKE query pattern
LimeSurvey documentation confirms 'responses' as valid permission type; export_result.sh queries WHERE permission='responses'
Task description 'View/Read responses'; LimeSurvey uses read_p column for read permissions; export_result.sh checks read_p=1

privileged_info_summary: Task is for LimeSurvey 6 running in Docker with MySQL 8.0 backend. All metadata claims verified: survey title is 'Consumer Brand Perception Study Q4 2024', question group is 'Brand Awareness' with minimum 2 questions required, two users to be created are j.martinez and r.nakamura with emails j.martinez@surveyresearchlab.org and r.nakamura@surveyresearchlab.org respectively. Both users must be granted 'responses' permission with 'read' access (read_p=1) for the specific survey only, preventing them from editing the survey structure. LimeSurvey permission system uses permission='responses' with read_p column to control response visibility. Data is synthetic and created during task execution via browser automation.
